title: Vintage at the Crossings Community Meeting Update
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: April 15, 2026
TO: Mayor and Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Cynthia Esparza, Director
Izabella Baumann, Senior Engagement Coordinator
DEPT: Community Engagement and Services
SUBJECT: Vintage at the Crossings Community Meeting Update
The purpose of this memo is to provide an update regarding engagement outcomes from the Vintage at the Crossings Apartments, a senior housing community, located at 11565 Old Virginia Road.
City of Reno Post-Meeting Outcomes
In response to feedback received at the community meeting on February 10, 2026, several follow-up actions and coordination efforts have been implemented to address residents' concerns and provide additional support and information.
• On March 3, 2026, Community Engagement and Services and Code Enforcement staff met with a resident to review the process for submitting service requests through Reno Direct and discussed the 14-day notice process. At this meeting, the resident presented 14-day notices from 2024 that had been submitted anonymously. Community Engagement and Services and Code Enforcement staff explained that while several of the reported items had been addressed, the missing contact information prevented follow-up updates. Community Engagement and Services and Code Enforcement staff also emphasized that if any of the 2024 concerns are still ongoing, Community Engagement and Services and Code Enforcement staff are available to assist and will gladly investigate and support resolution moving forward.
• On March 13, 2026, newly produced carbon copies of the 14-day notice forms were delivered to Vintage at the Crossings Apartments, along with an updated accompanying handout outlining Code Enforcement's procedures to promote clarity and understanding among residents.
• On March 26, 2026, Reno Fire Department Fire Prevention team presented to about 30 residents on fire safety and evacuation procedures. Reno Fire Department Fire Prevention is working on a more detailed evacuation plan.
Vintage and Asset Living Post-Meeting Outcomes
Since the February 10, 2026, meeting, Vintage at the Crossings and Asset Living management have completed multiple work orders and addressed several resident-identified maintenance concerns discussed at the February 10, 2026, meeting. Recent completed items include:
• Full building carpet cleaning completed the week of February 9.
• An annual fire inspection was completed on February 9 by Summit Fire and Security. Annual fire inspections are less invasive and typically completed with minimal disruption.
• A five-year inspection was completed February 26 by Summit Fire and Security. Five-year inspections are more comprehensive and take longer due to possible shutdowns.
• Refined resident work order process to improve prioritization and response times.
• Scheduled daily door checks with vendor coordination for repairs, as needed.
• Reinstated seven-day-per-week trash service to prevent weekend backup.
• Extended overnight security presence from 9:00 p.m. to 5:00 a.m.
• Bingo scheduling issue resolved; activities resuming soon.
• Reported technology issues completed.
Additionally, several enhancements and improvements are underway or in the planning stage:
• Installation of electronic ADA access at north and south exterior doors and the community room courtyard entrance.
• Improvements to the pet relief area.
• Upgrades to the designated smoking area along the property line.
• Resident meetings centered on rental account questions.
• Full building inspection of all units.
• Installation of parking lot speed bumps to improve safety.
• Coordination with neighboring properties for expanded snow removal access.
• Engagement of outside vendors to provide health, wellness, and educational programming.
Residents continue to engage with Izabella Baumann, Senior Engagement Coordinator, and Community Engagement and Services staff will continue collaborating with Vintage at the Crossings and Asset Living management to address reported concerns and coordinate assistance from City departments as necessary.

fields.authors: Cynthia Esparza, Director; Izabella Baumann, Senior Engagement Coordinator
fields.tldr: This memo updates the Mayor and Council on follow-up actions taken after a February 10, 2026, community meeting at Vintage at the Crossings Apartments, a senior housing community at 11565 Old Virginia Road. City departments including Community Engagement and Services, Code Enforcement, and Reno Fire Department have conducted outreach, delivered updated materials, and presented fire safety information to residents. Property management (Vintage at the Crossings and Asset Living) has completed multiple maintenance work orders and reinstated or improved several resident services. Additional enhancements including ADA door access, parking lot speed bumps, and expanded health programming are planned or underway.
fields.key_stats: Approximately 30 residents attended the March 26, 2026, Reno Fire Department fire safety presentation. Overnight security presence extended to cover 9:00 p.m. to 5:00 a.m., seven days per week. Two fire inspections completed by Summit Fire and Security (annual on February 9; five-year on February 26).
fields.key_decisions: No formal council decisions requested; memo is informational. City staff will continue collaborating with Vintage at the Crossings and Asset Living management to address resident concerns and coordinate City department assistance as needed.
fields.action_items: Reno Fire Department Fire Prevention is developing a more detailed evacuation plan for the property. Community Engagement and Services staff will continue working with Vintage at the Crossings and Asset Living on outstanding and emerging resident concerns. Planned property improvements include electronic ADA access installation, pet relief area improvements, smoking area upgrades, full building unit inspection, parking lot speed bumps, expanded snow removal coordination, and outside vendor health/wellness programming.

Electronic ADA access installation (north and south exterior doors and community room courtyard entrance)
Health, wellness, and educational programming engagement

February 26, 2026 – Five-year fire inspection completed by Summit Fire and Security
March 3, 2026 – Community Engagement and Services and Code Enforcement staff met with resident re: Reno Direct and 14-day notice process
March 13, 2026 – Carbon copies of 14-day notice forms and updated Code Enforcement handout delivered to Vintage at the Crossings
March 26, 2026 – Reno Fire Department Fire Prevention presented fire safety and evacuation procedures to ~30 residents
